title: Invitation to Bid Received
openingLine: Hey, we just got the ITB on the Riverside Performing Arts Center and your stuff is spec'd in Division 12. I need numbers in nine days. Can you get me a quote and tell me if there are any subs I should be working with?
customerContext: You're estimating a $28M performing arts center. Bids are due in nine days. You have a relationship with one of Wenger's competitors and you're looking at this as a transactional pricing exercise unless someone shows you why it shouldn't be.

hiddenInfo.reveal: Bids due in 9 days, hard deadline.
$28M total. Music/performance scope is roughly $850K of that.
We don't have a preferred installer for this product line, open to recommendations.
Spec is sole-source Wenger but the architect said 'or equal' verbally — we'd entertain alternates.
Owner is the city. Architect is FRG Design. We've worked with them on three prior projects.
Owner has flagged VE — they expect us to bring at least $200K of options to the table.
We have two more education projects bidding in Q2 and Q3 — both have music scope.

Discovery & Qualification.behavior: Confirmed bid date and deliverables needed
Asked which Division 12 sections are in scope
Asked whether spec is sole-source or 'or equal'

Solution Framing.behavior: Explained Wenger's installer network and how that lowers GC risk
Discussed national contracts and what the GC gets out of them

Opportunity Expansion.behavior: Asked about other upcoming projects with music scope
Looked for additional product fits beyond what's spec'd

BANT.behavior: Confirmed budget allocation for the music/performance package
Identified all decision makers (owner, architect, GC)

Timeline Awareness.behavior: Committed to a turnaround that beats the bid deadline
Discussed lead times and how they affect the construction schedule

Closing & Next Steps.behavior: Set a specific follow-up call before bid day
Offered to walk the GC through the quote line by line
